ucs.title: [LLM] Gallmaker DDE-borne Office macro-less execution spawning PowerShell reverse-shell tradecraft
ucs.description: Hunts Gallmaker's signature delivery chain: weaponized Office documents (geopolitical/defense lures) abusing the DDE protocol (T1559.002) instead of macros, where WINWORD/EXCEL spawn cmd.exe or powershell.exe with reverse-shell-style command lines (System.Net.Sockets.TCPClient, hidden window, encoded). This combination — DDE-as-execution + PowerShell reverse shell + Middle East defense targeting — is Gallmaker's documented tradecraft (Symantec, Oct 2018), distinct from generic macro-borne phishing.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(cmdline, "(?i)(TCPClient|System\.Net\.Sockets|GetStream|StreamReader|StreamWriter|-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?|-nop|-noni|-enc(odedcommand)?|FromBase64String|IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString|DownloadFile|cmd\.exe\s*/[ck])") | eval gallmaker_score = if(match(cmdline,"(?i)TCPClient|System\.Net\.Sockets"),3,0) + if(match(cmdline,"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"),1,0) + if(match(cmdline,"(?i)-enc(odedcommand)?|FromBase64String"),2,0) + if(match(cmdline,"(?i)IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString"),1,0) | where gallmaker_score >= 2 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - gallmaker_score, - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Gallmaker (G0084) — Office DDE → PowerShell reverse-shell chain
let Lookback = 14d;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let ShellChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
| where FileName in~ (ShellChildren)
// Gallmaker's reverse-shell PowerShell tradecraft + classic obfuscation flags
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| extend RevShellSig = cmd has_any ("tcpclient", "system.net.sockets", "getstream", "streamreader", "streamwriter")
| extend HiddenWin   = cmd matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
| extend EncodedCmd  = cmd has_any ("-enc ", "-encodedcommand", "-ec ", "frombase64string")
| extend Downloader  = cmd has_any ("downloadstring", "downloadfile", "invoke-webrequest", "iwr ", "iex ", "invoke-expression")
| extend NoProfile   = cmd has_any ("-nop", "-noprofile", "-noni", "-noninteractive")
| extend GallmakerScore = toint(RevShellSig)*3 + toint(HiddenWin) + toint(EncodedCmd)*2 + toint(Downloader) + toint(NoProfile)
| where GallmakerScore >= 2 or RevShellSig == true   // RevShell alone is enough
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation,
          GallmakerScore, RevShellSig, HiddenWin, EncodedCmd, Downloader
| order by GallmakerScore desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Gallmaker (G0084) — Office DDE → PowerShell reverse-shell chain (Sentinel/SecurityEvent 4688)
let Lookback = 14d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("\\winword.exe", "\\excel.exe", "\\powerpnt.exe", "\\outlook.exe")
| where NewProcessName has_any ("\\cmd.exe", "\\powershell.exe", "\\pwsh.exe", "\\mshta.exe", "\\rundll32.exe", "\\regsvr32.exe", "\\wscript.exe", "\\cscript.exe")
| extend cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
| extend RevShellSig = cmd has_any ("tcpclient", "system.net.sockets", "getstream", "streamreader", "streamwriter")
| extend HiddenWin   = cmd matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
| extend EncodedCmd  = cmd has_any ("-enc ", "-encodedcommand", "-ec ", "frombase64string")
| extend Downloader  = cmd has_any ("downloadstring", "downloadfile", "invoke-webrequest", "iwr ", "iex ", "invoke-expression")
| extend NoProfile   = cmd has_any ("-nop", "-noprofile", "-noni", "-noninteractive")
| extend GallmakerScore = toint(RevShellSig)*3 + toint(HiddenWin) + toint(EncodedCmd)*2 + toint(Downloader) + toint(NoProfile)
| where GallmakerScore >= 2 or RevShellSig == true
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, AccountDomain,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, NewProcessId,
          GallmakerScore, RevShellSig, HiddenWin, EncodedCmd, Downloader
| order by GallmakerScore desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Gallmaker DDE-Borne Office Spawning PowerShell Reverse-Shell Tradecraft
id: 6f9d2c1a-4b8e-4a2c-9c1d-b5a8f3d2e1a4
status: experimental
description: Detects Gallmaker (G0084) signature chain — Microsoft Office process spawning a script interpreter with command-line markers of a PowerShell reverse shell, hidden window, base64 encoding, or in-memory downloader, consistent with the DDE-protocol abuse documented by Symantec (Oct 2018).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0084/
  - https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/threat-intelligence/gallmaker-attack-group
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1559.002
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0084
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  selection_revshell:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'TCPClient'
      - 'System.Net.Sockets'
      - 'GetStream'
      - 'StreamReader'
      - 'StreamWriter'
  selection_evasion:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-WindowStyle Hidden'
      - '-w hidden'
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - '-enc '
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'IEX'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and (selection_revshell or selection_evasion)
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IT scripts launched from Office add-ins (rare; should be allowlisted by parent path/SHA256)
  - Security training / red-team exercises
level: high
ucs.rationale: Gallmaker is uniquely characterized in public reporting (Symantec, Oct 2018) for forgoing custom malware and instead weaponizing Office documents that abuse the DDE protocol (T1559.002) to spawn PowerShell reverse shells against Middle Eastern defense, military, and government entities. Generic 'Office spawns PowerShell' rules over-fire on macro phishing of every flavor; this rule narrows on Gallm
